item.title: Little Finder Guy Stars in Nine New Videos on TikTok and YouTube 摘要
item.description:
Apple has released nine Little Finder Guy videos this week on TikTok and YouTube, where the thumbnails form a mosaic on Apple's TikTok page. The author expresses hope to feature Little Finder Guy as a guest on The Talk Show Live From WWDC in June, while noting concerns about potentially jinxing negotiations.
标签: Apple, Little Finder Guy, TikTok, YouTube, WWDC, The Talk Show, marketing, videos, social media, tech news

item.title: Prototyping with LLMs 摘要
item.description:
The article discusses the temptation to immediately prototype with LLMs due to their ease of use, but warns this can lead to confusion about project goals. It suggests sketching as a faster, cheaper alternative that helps clarify ideas before committing resources. The author references a biblical parable about planning before building to emphasize the importance of upfront thinking in the design process.
标签: prototyping, LLM, design process, sketching, planning, productivity, AI tools, creative workflow, efficiency, project management

item.title: Anthropic Accidentally Leaked the Entire Claude Code CLI Source Code 摘要
item.description:
Anthropic accidentally leaked the entire source code for Claude Code CLI (nearly 2,000 TypeScript files, over 512,000 lines) by including a source map file in version 2.1.88 of the npm package. Security researcher Chaofan Shou first disclosed the leak on X, leading to widespread forking on GitHub. Anthropic acknowledged it as a packaging error from human error, not a security breach, and stated no customer data or credentials were exposed. However, this incident undermines trust in Claude Code's ability to handle sensitive source code securely.
标签: security, AI, data-leak, Anthropic, Claude, npm, TypeScript, source-code, GitHub, packaging-error

item.title: [Sponsor] Zed, a Font Superfamily 摘要
item.description:
Zed is a type system designed for optimal readability, developed by testing with visually impaired patients at a French ophthalmology hospital where Zed Text outperformed Helvetica in reading speed across all groups. It features two optical versions (Text and Display), four variable axes, and supports 547 languages including endangered ones. The font is available directly from its designers.
标签: typography, accessibility, font-design, readability, visual-impairment, multilingual, variable-fonts, design, user-testing, open-type
